claim: Canada's restrained-reordering coding assumes a stable sacred political order, but the ongoing Quebec sovereignty crisis and unresolved questions of Indigenous rights and treaty obligations indicate a sacred political order under chronic contestation that may be better coded as hybrid-transitional or stagnation.
rationale: The Canadian sacred political order has not achieved the kind of settled corrigibility that postwar Germany or Switzerland exhibit — its restrained-reordering is internally contested and periodically destabilized by nationalist and Indigenous mobilization.
